title: harness-lock
description: Schema for `.harness-lock.json`. This file is written by `harness sync` and read by `harness sync --check` for drift detection. It captures the exact harness ref, resolved SHA, and per-file hashes so the check command can identify out-of-band edits.

harness_ref.description: The git ref the lock was synced from — a semver tag (e.g. `v0.1.0`), a branch name, or a full 40-character commit SHA. This is the ref passed to `harness sync --ref`. Used for display and audit purposes.

resolved_sha.description: The 40-character lowercase hexadecimal git commit SHA that `harness_ref` resolved to at sync time. This is the authoritative version identifier used in drift comparisons — if `resolved_sha` in the lock file does not match the harness ref's current HEAD, the consumer is out of date.

config_schema_version.description: The `$id` URI (or semver subset) of `harness.config.schema.json` that was active when this lock file was written. Allows the sync engine to detect when the consumer's harness.config.json was validated against an older schema version and a re-validation or migration may be needed.

synced_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp recording when `harness sync` last ran (UTC recommended, e.g. `2026-05-03T05:30:00Z`). INFORMATIONAL ONLY — this field MUST NOT be used in drift comparisons. Its value changes on every sync regardless of whether any managed content changed; using it for drift detection would produce false positives on every sync invocation.

files.description: One lock record per file processed during the last `harness sync` run, in the order they were processed. Each record captures the file class, hashes, and per-block state needed for `harness sync --check` drift detection.

scaffolds.description: Ordered list of scaffolds applied during the last `harness sync` run, each pinned to a specific version. Empty array if no scaffolds were applied.

excluded.description: Relative paths (from the consumer repo root) of project-owned files that are explicitly excluded from sync. The sync engine skips these paths entirely. Examples: `README.md`, `LICENSE`, `package.json`, `.gitignore`, `.editorconfig`.

items.description: Relative path from the consumer repo root to a file excluded from `harness sync` (e.g. `README.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`).

blockEntry.description: Lock record for a single composed block within a `composed`-class file. A composed block is a region in the rendered file delimited by opening and closing template marker lines; the body between the markers is preserved across syncs and may be edited by the consumer developer.

id.description: Block identifier as declared in the template marker (e.g. `custom-ci-steps`, `project.env`). Must begin with a lowercase ASCII letter and contain only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens, and dots. Matches the regex `[a-z][a-z0-9.-]*`.

source_line_range.description: 1-based, inclusive line range in the rendered output file (after templating) that this block occupies, including the opening and closing marker lines. Used to locate the block during re-sync without re-parsing.

start.description: Line number of the opening marker line in the rendered output file (1-based, inclusive). Must be less than or equal to `end`.

end.description: Line number of the closing marker line in the rendered output file (1-based, inclusive). Must be greater than or equal to `start`.

body_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the block body — the bytes between the opening and closing template marker lines, exclusive of the marker lines themselves. This hash is the primary signal for per-block drift detection: if it changes between syncs, `harness sync --check` reports that the user has edited (or a tool has overwritten) the block body.

template_marker_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the concatenated opening and closing template marker lines (in rendered form, after variable substitution). Detects changes to the marker syntax or identifiers without requiring a full re-render — for example, if the block ID is renamed in the template.

provenance.description: How the block body came to be: `user-authored` — the body was written or last substantively edited by the consumer developer (body_hash does not match the seeded placeholder); `seeded-empty` — the block body was seeded as an empty or placeholder stub and has not yet been edited by the developer; `migrated-from-legacy` — the body was migrated from a pre-marker-format file during a `harness sync --migrate` run and has not yet been confirmed by the developer.

fileEntry.description: Lock record for a single file processed by `harness sync`. The `class` field determines which drift-detection strategy applies and whether `blocks` is present.

target.description: Relative path from the consumer repo root to the file written (or skipped) by `harness sync` (e.g. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `CONVENTIONS.md`).

source_template.description: Relative path from the harness repo root to the template file that was rendered to produce `target` (e.g. `template/managed/.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `template/composed/CONVENTIONS.md`).

class.description: File class as declared in the consumer's `harness.config.json`: `managed` — the file is fully overwritten on every sync; any out-of-band edits are considered drift; `composed` — the file has a managed template core with marker-preserved local extension blocks; the blocks are carried forward across syncs; `seeded` — the file is written once (if missing) and never overwritten; the consumer owns it after creation.

rendered_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the post-templating bytes actually written to disk for `target`. For `managed` files, `harness sync --check` compares the current on-disk hash against this value to detect out-of-band edits. For `composed` files, this covers the full rendered file including all block contents. For `seeded` files, this is the hash at creation time (not re-checked on subsequent syncs).

action.description: Action taken on this file during the last `harness sync` run: `created` — file did not exist and was written for the first time; `updated` — file existed and was overwritten with newly rendered content; `skipped` — rendered content was identical to what was already on disk (hash match); no write performed; `preserved` — file is `seeded`-class and already existed; left untouched; `excluded` — file path appears in the `excluded` list and was skipped entirely.

blocks.description: Per-block lock records for `composed`-class files. REQUIRED when `class` is `composed`; MUST be absent when `class` is `managed` or `seeded`. Each entry records the block ID, line range, and hashes needed to detect drift in individual blocks without re-rendering the whole file.

template_prose_hash.description: Optional. SHA-256 hash of the template's skeleton (post-templating, post-local-block-strip, LF-normalised) at the last sync. Composed-class only. When present, mergeComposed() uses it to distinguish 'template prose evolved' (the template's prose changed but the consumer didn't touch their prose — auto-adopt the new template prose) from 'consumer edited prose' (the consumer made out-of-band edits to non-block content — fall through to the existing fail-closed legacy_composed_mapping path). Absent on locks written by harness < v0.2.0; the engine bootstraps on first encounter (silently auto-adopts current template prose and records the hash for the next sync). Per CS03d / LRN-020.

scaffoldEntry.description: A scaffold applied during the last `harness sync` run, identified by name and pinned to a specific version.

name.description: Human-readable scaffold identifier as declared in `harness.config.json` (e.g. `node-esm-cli`, `github-actions-ci`). Must be unique within the `scaffolds` array.

version.description: Version of the scaffold at sync time — a semver string (e.g. `1.2.0`) or a git ref (e.g. `v0.1.0`, full SHA). Recorded so `harness sync --check` can detect when the pinned scaffold version has changed.
